scenarios.title: Document Processing, Unstructured Data, and RAG Preparation
scenarios.when_to_read: Document conversion, partitioning, OCR/table pipelines, Markdown conversion, document elements, chunking, extraction, cleaning, staging, remote document services, and RAG-ready document preparation.
scenarios.role: Use the Unstructured Python package to partition documents into elements, inspect element metadata, chunk outputs for RAG, clean/stage data, add embeddings, evaluate extraction quality, and diagnose optional dependency readiness.
scenarios.read_when: The request names `unstructured` or asks for package-specific APIs, CLIs, configs, data/model artifacts, error messages, workflows, or repository maintenance that match this project.
scenarios.best_for: chunking, data preparation, elements and metadata, embeddings, evaluation, and partitioning.
scenarios.avoid_when: another repo skill in this scenario matches the user's task, package, model family, data format, serving backend, or workflow more directly.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `unstructured` when the request names `unstructured`, centers on RAG, clean/stage data, add embeddings, evaluate extraction quality, and diagnose optional dependency readiness, uses its APIs or CLIs, references its configs/artifacts/errors, or asks for repository workflows in document processing unstructured data and rag preparation. Then route to chunking, data preparation, elements and metadata, or embeddings when those focused workflows match.
